title: crambe orientalis var. alutacea
intro: Crambe orientalis var. alutacea, also known as sea kale, is a variety of perennial plant that belongs to the Brassica family, characterized by its edible shoots and leaves. The medicinal properties of Crambe orientalis var. alutacea include its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome. The health benefits of consuming this plant include its potential to improve gut health, boost the immune system, and provide essential vitamins and minerals. Crambe orientalis var. alutacea can be prepared as a vegetable, salad, or herbal supplement, and its shoots and leaves can also be used to make teas, infusions, and other herbal preparations.
